content:
We've had the *Aliemus*/*Alienus* debate for english, the studio decided that "Aliemus" is dear to their hearths.
Great !
Does that apply to all languages ?
Should French players charge up their exalts with *Aliemus*, or with something else that might make a little more sense to them (be it a literal translation of madness like the mtl is sometimes doing, or a *fancy* term) ?

More generally, what terms should we **not touch** for languages besides english ?
<@1376763322568937562> 

-# See <@145243403083317249>'s interrogations there: https://discord.com/channels/1402537664619479100/1402539609107464212/1415007738567594187

Hello, member of the French section here. I have a question about the Aliemus term : I need to understand the word "Aliemus" because in France,  alot of words design the "foolish", but also the 'Madness' that is same but different.

您好，我是法语翻译团队的成员之一。
请帮我理解一下“狂气”这个词。在英语中，它变成了“Aliemus”，但在法语中，很多词都指同一个意思。
_
Also, we would like to know the words and terms we shouldn't touch.

此外，我们还想知道哪些词语和术语是我们永远不应该触碰的。

Question for <@1376763322568937562>:
I'd like to make liberal use of newlines and spaces to help clean up gameplay features.
*(for examples, putting \n at the end of card text to separate card mechanics from affixes like Exhaust, and spaces before affix strings to avoid long run-on concatenated affix chains)*
I've seen examples of both the newline character `\n` and actual line breaks in different places, and I'm wondering if one or the other is preferred for any reason, or if they're rendered differently at all.
I also have a concern that spaces at the beginnings and ends of text strings could end up getting culled by accident at some point in the process of implementing the text into the game proper (excel really likes doing this automatically...) 

Would you know if any of these things are a concern?

Hello! Yes, you can add 
 or change where a 
 is placed in your translation; just be sure you’re not adding too many (it may break the interface) or pressing enter instead of adding 
 (true line changes breaks the sheet)!

oh hey, thanks for the quick response! so to be clear, you said true line changes will break the sheet?
In that case, should cells like these be fixed to use \n instead of true line changes?
They appear to render fine in-game, but in the case that we edit these cells we should avoid having line breaks like these go back upstream to biav?

Yeah sometimes our other devs went lazy... They should've used \n here! If it's working, should be fine to keep it as is;

We recommend \n because before the translation is implemented in-game, it is first uploaded as a csv, and true line breaks could go wrong in that process.
so to be absolutely clear : **no line breaks should ever exist, we should always use "\n" to symbolize a line break**. Right ?

<@1376763322568937562> 
sorry forgot to ping with the reply
Sorry! I was hospitalized on that day and didn't see your ping

Indeed, it's a tricky issue, as different cultures express closeness and use honorifics differently.

In the original Chinese, Keeper calls most people directly by their name to sound easy-going and direct. In contrast, Hameln uses "Mr." or "Miss" to sound formal, polite, and elegant. This intentional choice does make him seem a bit cold and distant, which reflects his personality.

While I don't speak German and can't advise on the best translation approach, I hope this insight into the Chinese version is helpful!
Also I'm glad to have learned more about the German language from you guys!! It's interesting

That helps a ton! I'll switch Keeper to informal then while keeping Hameln formal, since with the added background it does make a lot of sense. 

Thank you for your help and I wish you all the best health-wise
